target: SEC-4.6 Registry Framing Audit — update TLDR and row-2 of six-concession table to capture (a) wins.html visible grid drift 9→10 since ISS-1132 filing 2026-04-18, (b) predictions.html banner publishing a fourth value (12) with no caveat, (c) overview.json explicit 'display-level bookkeeping' admission as kernel-of-truth. Close ISS-1132 cluster.
curmudgeon_review: n/a — analyst-baby orphan pickup (ISS-1132), not a curmudgeon-authored review

patches.old_text: The dome's machine-readable API layer (methodology.json, claim_index.json, ai_manifest.json) now publishes concessions that the visible homepage and dashboard don't reflect. We treat the concessions as genuine rigor improvements and audit only the remaining divergence — most sharply, a three-layer disagreement on the prospective_confirmed sub-count (10 / 9 / 1) — each publication of the same aggregate gives a different number.
patches.new_text: The dome's machine-readable API layer (methodology.json, claim_index.json, ai_manifest.json, overview.json) now publishes concessions that the visible homepage and dashboard don't reflect. We treat the concessions as genuine rigor improvements and audit only the remaining divergence — most sharply, a four-layer disagreement on the prospective_confirmed sub-count (10 / 10 / 1 / 12) where the dome's own machine layer labels two of the four as "display-level bookkeeping" and the visible predictions banner labels the fourth without any caveat at all.

patches.anchor: details id="registry-framing-audit" > div.ps-detail > table.gap-audit-table > tbody > tr (second row, prospective_confirmed = 10 row)
patches.old_text: prospective_confirmed = 10 in ai_manifest.jsonVisible grid on homepage shows 9 prospective-confirmed items; claim_index.json strict subset counts 1Three different publications of the same aggregate disagree: 10 / 9 / 1. Whichever is correct, at least two are wrong. (Newly-discovered; not previously tracked in our issue queue.)
patches.new_text: prospective_confirmed = 10 in ai_manifest.json; prospective_display = 10 in overview.json (the latter explicitly labels itself 'display-level bookkeeping' in its registry_semantics block)wins.html visible Prospective Wins grid shows 10 cards as of 2026-05-16 (was 9 when this row was first audited on 2026-04-18 — the grid grew by one without any of the four published aggregate numbers moving); claim_index.json strict subset counts 1 (only pred-eclipse-tier3); predictions.html top-of-page banner publishes a fourth number, 12 PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED, with the explicit roster W009 / W010 / W011 / W013 / W015 / WIN-059 / WIN-060 / WIN-061 / SAA separation / NP acceleration / Roaring 40s / WIN-069 — and that banner carries no caveat about display-vs-audit semanticsFour publications of the same nominal aggregate now disagree: 10 / 10 / 1 / 12 (with a separate 36-card "Prospective Weekly Confirmations" section on wins.html that is a different metric but uses confusingly similar wording). The two-machine-layer 10s genuinely agree, the strict claim-by-claim audit gives 1, and the predictions banner — the most user-visible of all four surfaces — gives 12. The author has admitted in overview.json that the display number is bookkeeping; that admission has not propagated to predictions.html, which still presents 12 as a scientific aggregate. Whichever number is correct, at least two of the four are wrong by the author's own published definitions.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's machine layer is meaningfully more honest than its visible layer. overview.json's registry_semantics block explicitly says the display count is "Display-level registry ratio shown on the public site" and "Not a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective scoring" — that is genuinely correct epistemology, and the author put it in the API on his own initiative. ai_manifest.json says the same with "Do not treat the display ratio as a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective audit." An AI agent or researcher reading the API gets a fair picture: the headline is bookkeeping, the audited subset is 1. The critique is narrower than "the author is misleading readers" — it is "the author is honest in one channel and silent in the louder channel." predictions.html's 12-item banner is the loudest channel of all, and it carries none of the machine-layer caveats.
holes_addressed: Stale grid count (9 → 10) carried in our SEC-4.6 row 2 since 2026-04-18.
Missing fourth layer (predictions.html banner = 12) not previously documented in our audit despite being the most visible surface.

Kernel-of-truth understatement — we said the API layer is "substantively more rigorous" without quoting overview.json's explicit "display-level" admission verbatim, which would strengthen the credit-where-credit-is-due framing.
new_evidence_added: overview.json registry_semantics block (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/overview.json, last_updated 2026-04-17) — explicit display-vs-audit limit
predictions.html banner with explicit 12-item roster (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html line ~31)
wins.html h3-card recount today (2026-05-16): 10 cards in Prospective Wins section, up from 9 at ISS-1132 filing

anticipated_objections.objection: The predictions.html banner counts a different population — items that have been confirmed as predictions, not all prospective-tagged WINs — so the 12 is not in the same denominator as the 10 and the disagreement is illusory.
anticipated_objections.response: That defense is exactly what overview.json's registry_semantics block recommends: don't confuse display counts with audited counts. If the 12 is a different population ("predictions that confirmed" rather than "items currently tagged prospective in the registry"), the banner should say so — and it doesn't. The banner reads "PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED: 12 predictions confirmed before data came in," which any reader will fold back into the same prospective category that ai_manifest.json publishes as 10. The disagreement is between what the banner means and what an ordinarily-literate reader will infer. If the author's intended meaning is narrower, the fix is one sentence of disambiguation on the banner; the absence of that sentence is the gap we document.

anticipated_objections.objection: The grid drift from 9 to 10 is just an entry being added in normal registry maintenance — it's not evidence of inconsistency.
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed in isolation, and we don't treat it as evidence of dishonesty. We treat it as evidence that none of the four aggregate numbers moved in sync with the grid that allegedly underlies them: ai_manifest stayed at 10, overview stayed at 10, predictions banner stayed at 12, strict audit stayed at 1. If the four numbers were derived from the grid, at least one of them should have moved when the grid grew by one. They didn't, so they aren't derived from the grid — they're hand-curated literals that happen to be near the grid. That is the load-bearing observation, not the +1.

anticipated_objections.objection: Critiquing a count drift of 1 is petty.
anticipated_objections.response: We agree it would be, if the count drift were the whole critique. It isn't. The whole critique is that the author publishes four different aggregate counts of the same nominal quantity on four different public surfaces, three of those four are static literals not derived from the underlying grid, and the most visible surface carries none of the caveats that the least-visible one carries. The +1 is a footnote, not the headline.

proposed_followup_issues.title: Poller hook: daily-diff prospective_confirmed across all four dome layers
proposed_followup_issues.description: Add a hook to monitor.py / pull_data.py to record ai_manifest.json:counts.prospective_confirmed, overview.json:current_state.prospective_display, claim_index.json strict-prospective count (filter claims by status=="prospective"), predictions.html banner regex match (/PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED:\s*(\d+)/), and wins.html h3-count in the Prospective Wins block. Daily snapshot to a small JSON, and flag in the next poll note if any of the four numbers move. Closes the testability ask in ISS-1132 ("add poller hook to daily-diff…"). The original ISS-1132 ask tied the hook to schema-extension landing — which is no longer a precondition; the four-layer audit can be polled today.

summary_for_decider: Closes ISS-1132 via SEC-4.6 row-2 + TLDR patch. Two surgical text edits inside data/sections.json part4. Material new content: (1) wins.html grid grew 9→10 since ISS-1132 filing without any of the four published aggregates moving, (2) predictions.html banner adds a fourth disagreement (12) with no caveat, (3) overview.json explicit display-vs-audit limit quoted as kernel-of-truth credit to the author. Also proposes one follow-up tooling issue for a poller hook. After integration, the SEC-4.6 row-2 cell-text will be longer than the original (~270 words → ~360 words) — within section's existing per-cell variance.
test_recommendations: After decider integration, run tests/run.sh (full). The grep-based prose tests are unlikely to fire on these numeric changes — the old "10 / 9 / 1" string isn't asserted anywhere I found — but the JSON-shape and table-row-count tests should still pass.

Suggested ADD: a new test asserting the SEC-4.6 row-2 cell contains all four current numbers (10, 10, 1, 12) and explicitly references overview.json and predictions.html banner. This is the "new numeric claim → add a test" rule from CLAUDE.md applied to this expansion.
